{{Image Banner|image=Level_18_repeating.png|caption='''DAISY, DAISY...'''}}
{{Blurquote|quote-text=
{{Class Custom Image|hexagon=Ψ|safety=Safe|security=Unstable|color-h=30|color-s=100%|color-l=29%|entity=Moderate Mental Hazards|text-color=FFFFFF|class=Ψ|bg-image=Level18.jpeg}}
'''Level 18''' is the '''19th''' level of '''[[The Backrooms]]'''.|image=Level18.jpeg|person-font-size=0}}{{Blurquote|quote-text= ''DESCRIPTION'' |image=Level18.jpeg|size=2000px}}
{{Blurimage|file=Level18.jpeg|caption='''One specific wanderer's memories included this kindergarten classroom.'''}} '''Level 18''' presents itself in a unique form to each wanderer. It's similar to a normal kindergarten and also appears as a lifeless, eerie recreation of the wanderer's memories from early adolescence. The environments at this level vary greatly, as one would expect, manifesting either indoors or outdoors and representing various places from the wanderer's past, with major examples including homes, schools, parks, cars, and other significant locations. However, these memories appear to be completely devoid of life, with only occasional microorganisms and plants present. Furthermore, these memories seem to be "tampered" with, not manifesting exactly as the wanderers remember. It is said that some people may feel dissociated from themselves if they don't have fond memories of their past. Memories are separate from one another and can be accessed at will. They are suspended in a black void, where the wanderer can jump from one memory to another. The void's low gravity makes traversing it effortless, but this is not the case for the memories themselves. It feels almost as if one could be hallucinating, just like any similar facade in the backrooms. Consequently, places that do not resemble this level's appearance often cause suffering. Upon first entering this level, the wanderer experiences a wave of nostalgia and happiness, reminiscing about the good times they had in these places. However, the isolation of this level gradually takes hold as each wanderer has their own memories, making it impossible for two wanderers to meet within the same memory. {{Blurimage|file=d722a965a5a6d16be838aa06c1408007.jpg|caption='''Another photograph of a wanderer's memory, resembling a school hallway.'''|align=left}} The isolation of this level starts to take a toll on one's sanity as they wander through their memories, searching for an exit. Rumors have it that some people feel anxious about their past, considering the things they've done. This level typically evokes a sense of hopelessness, making the wanderer believe that there is no exit. Exiting this level presents a challenge, as one must drink [[Object 1|Almond Water]] constantly to stay focused and avoid giving up. [[Object 1|Almond Water bottles]] can be found floating in the void, and jumping to them is necessary to keep going. Attempting a large jump in the void and failing results in a ten-minute fall, accompanied by intense pain, before waking up at the starting point. It is recommended not to attempt such a jump if one is not physically fit. This level has no connection to the outside world, such as the internet or a signal. Bringing anything from this level outside is impossible, as it disappears as soon as it leaves the level. For some unknown reason, the only items that can be taken from this level are photographs. {{Blurimage|file=8bb4148cd34709ce5eb999882fb0fe17 (1).jpg|caption='''One of the first photos of this level.'''}} This level appears to be as ancient as the Backrooms themselves, having been discovered in 1930 by a wanderer named Ronald M. His memories of the level depicted an old house and a park. However, soon after, the wanderer fell into the void and woke up where they had first fallen asleep. While in this level, wanderers are unable to fall asleep, no matter how hard they try, nor can they fall unconscious. One is always awake in this level, though wanderers may become increasingly fatigued. If one is exceptionally tired, it is recommended to simply jump into the void. Numerous rumors abound about various hallucinations that can occur in this level, ranging from auditory to visual and even to the sudden flooding of past memories. These hallucinations are typically regarded as a part of '''The Paranoia Effect''', which intensifies as time goes on. Entities are commonly encountered in this level, but they pose little danger to wanderers. If a wanderer catches sight of one, the entity will pursue them but will not cause any harm. Instead, it will simply follow them for a while. It is during this time that wanderers can meet and form colonies, but these groups are often fleeting and broken due to the level's complex, unstable nature. After some time, '''The Paranoia Effect''' will begin to manifest, causing the level and one's memories to become increasingly dark and obscure, to the point where nothing is visible without a source of light. The longer one spends in this level, the more likely they are to experience glitches and encounter visual anomalies. While not inherently dangerous, these glitches can result in the duplication of memories in the void, which poses a risk of falling through. As wanderers descend further into madness, their memories will shift into traumatic recollections, perpetuating the cycle of '''The Paranoia Effect.''' Notes found by unknown authors further lead to the effects of '''The Paranoia Effect''', to be true. {{Blurquote|image=D66861361905782a308ba8d2981bfc78.jpg|quote-text="As I reminisce about my past, a sense of nostalgia overcomes me. However, I cannot deny that I am firmly rooted in the present. Although it is almost as if I've been transported back to my preschool days, something feels off; the environment seems askew, as if the objects are suspended in a void. This eerie feeling of emptiness bounces throughout the atmosphere, compounding the already-existing disorientation."}}
{{Blurquote|quote-text= ''THE PARANOIA EFFECT'' |image=Level18.jpeg|size=2000px}}
{{Blurimage|file=static-assets-upload14123703834999409222.webp|caption='''"Amber Alert..."'''|align=left}} The "Paranoia Effect" stage is the most noteworthy aspect of this level. It begins shortly after a day or two of travel, when memories and surroundings become so black that nothing can be seen without a lantern. Entities arrive with increasing frequency, although they pose no significant harm to wanderers. These entities might pursue a wanderer who might catch their attention, but they don't intend to harm them and simply follow them for a brief period of time. This phase is distinguished by wanderers establishing colonies, though after a while they become unable to accumulate materials in a single __cpLocation for an extended period, and then can do so again. It's an elaborate and disconnected system as a whole, just like the level itself. As time passes, the level begins to show signs of instability. Though not entirely dangerous, there is a possibility that the memories may duplicate in the void, and said duplicates seem to lack a physical form, thus causing wanderers to fall into the void. Furthermore, it is common for the pleasant memories of the wanderers to shift into traumatic ones as their mental well-being worsens.
{{Blurquote|quote-text= ''THEORIES'' |image=Level18.jpeg|size=2000px}}
===
'''THE DREAM THEORY'''
=== Due to the level's odd quality of resembling a childhood memory and the fact that the only method of entry is sleep, it is frequently debated whether this level truly exists or is simply a shared dream among many wanderers. As additional backing for this claim, when the user supposedly "exits" the level, they wake up exactly where they fell asleep. Wanderers accompanying sleeping individuals who claimed to have entered '''Level 18''' have reported no unusual behavior during sleeping hours. With the additional revelation that time passing in '''Level 18''' has no correlation to time passing in the real world, we are leaning toward the conclusion that '''Level 18''' is not a physical place; it is just a mental image made up in wanderers' heads. ===
'''THE TORMENT THEORY'''
=== Various wanderers claim that this level is a sick punishment given by the Backrooms to wanderers; however, there is not much evidence that supports this theory.
{{Blurquote|quote-text= ''COLONIES AND OUTPOSTS'' |image=Level18.jpeg|size=2000px}}
{{Collapse|num=1|initial=
'''"Due to the nature of this level, colonies and outposts are unable to be established."'''
|final=
'''''"You couldn't care about anyone besides your parents. They tried to wish you luck here, but you've ended up here; now, you can never see them again. At least there are more people like you that ended up here; why not meet up with them?"'''''
|body= ===
'''THE LOST CREW'''
=== *Consists of about 28 members *Happy to feel their past memories *Willing to guide you through ===
'''UPSET TEENAGERS'''
=== *Feel bad for their "terrible" future *Wanted to stay here for their memories *Upset for all pain they've felt }}
{{Blurquote|quote-text= ''ENTRANCES AND EXITS''|image=Level18.jpeg|size=2000px}}
===
'''ENTRANCES'''
=== *Sleeping in [[Level 17|Level 17]] will take one here. *Entering the Greenhouse in [[Level 897]] while it has yellow lights will take one here.
'''EXITS'''
*Fall in the void and you'll wake up exactly where you slept when entering the level. *Entering a strange place resembling a dark hallway will bring you to [[Level 19|Level 19]]. *Noclipping in a memory resembling a hospital will lead one to [[Level !]]. This has later been deemed impossible as of the current, as trying to enter said level will lead to [[Level 492|Level 492]] instead. * Imagining a high school will bring you to [[Level 956]]. * Going up Stairs with a green Carped on every Stair leads you to [[Level 56|Level 56]]. * Certain indoor areas in this level will lead one to [[Level Fun]]. * Imagining a dystopian city will lead one to [[Level 82]]. * Finding a certain painting that resembles a greyscale land and slamming your head into said painting will send you to [[Level 142|Level 142]]. * Opening and picking up an 'I Spy' book and reading it will send one to [[Level 18.85]]. * Imagining a theater or Blockbuster, or similar establishments, will send one to [[Level 40]]. * Rarely, doors will lead to outdoor, wintery areas that transition into [[Level 393]]. * Having bad dreams frequently may transport one to [[Level 336]].
